title: Ipomoea cernua
taxonomy: Ipomoea cernua is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Ipomoea and the section Quamoclit. The classification of Ipomoea cernua is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Solanales, family Convolvulaceae, genus Ipomoea, species I. cernua. This species is also known as the winter morning glory or purple winter morning glory. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the morning glory family.
uses: Ipomoea cernua, also known as the common morning glory, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant contains compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use. The plant's potential medicinal uses warrant further investigation and study.
benefits: Ipomoea cernua, commonly known as the common morning glory or upright morning glory, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to possess antispasmodic, antitussive, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. Additionally, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and pain. Its extracts are also thought to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, making it potentially useful in treating infections and promoting overall well-being. Further research is needed to fully understand and confirm the health benefits of Ipomoea cernua.

parts: Ipomoea cernua, also known as the common morning glory, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The roots and seeds of this plant are considered medicinal parts. side_effects: Ipomoea cernua, also known as the common morning glory or purple morning glory, can cause side effects in some individuals. The plant's seeds contain a toxic compound called damsin, which can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain if ingested. Additionally, the plant's flowers and leaves contain alkaloids that can be toxic to humans and pets if ingested in large quantities. Ingestion of Ipomoea cernua can also lead to hallucinations and delirium due to its psychoactive properties. If handled improperly, the plant's sap can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some people.
intro: Ipomoea cernua, also known as the trumpet vine or cernua morning glory, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and twining stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, promoting sleep, and alleviating symptoms of menopause due to its estrogenic and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea cernua include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ipomoea cernua is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in capsule and tablet form, to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
